title: caltha palustris f. czarnohorensis
intro: Caltha palustris f. czarnohorensis, also known as Marsh Marigold or Caltha palustris, is a flowering plant species within the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its active compounds, including glycosides and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal medicine, Caltha palustris f. czarnohorensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
